title: MUS 218 - Keyboard Musicianship
description: Musical notation, rhythm, meter, key signatures, intervals, major and minor scales, intervals, triads and seventh chords, and their practical applications on the piano keyboard.

title: MUS 113 - Piano I
description: Class piano studies for music majors.

title: MUS 130 - Piano II
description: Class instruction for non-piano emphasis music majors.

title: MUS 330 - Piano III
description: Piano accompaniments developed from chord symbols, notated melodies, or choral and instrumental music. Chord voicing, chord analysis, stylistic appropriateness.

title: MUS 219 - Foundations of Harmony
description: Principles of diatonic and chromatic harmony.  Chord functions, chord spelling, lead sheets, and harmonic analysis of musical examples.

title: MUS 341 - Harmonic Connections: Voice Leading
description: Principles of voice leading and chord function applied to two-voice counterpoint and four-voice chorale-style and keyboard-style writing.

title: MUS 342 - Form and Analysis
description: Analysis of musical forms - motives, phrases, invention, binary and ternary form, variation, rondo, fugue, sonata Form, isorhythmic motet, Romantic song forms, blues, song forms in popular music.

title: MUS 343 - Post-Tonal Theory
description: th century techniques: tone rows, set theory, quartal harmony, polytonality, pandiatonicism, chance operations, modal writing, polymeters, and asymmetric meters.

title: MUS 344 - Jazz Harmony in Theory and Practice
description: Major principles and concepts of jazz: jazz rhythm and various approaches to jazz harmony, including functional harmony, and blues and modal theories.

title: MUS 216 - Ear Training I
description: Comprehensive ear training correlated to MUS 218; develop music reading and perception skills through studies in rhythm, sight singing, dictation, keyboard, and notation.

title: MUS 217 - Ear Training II
description: Continue development of skills introduced in Ear Training I.

title: MUS 316 - Ear Training III
description: Develop music reading and perception skills through studies in rhythm (traditional, 20th century), sight singing (traditional, 20th century), dictation, and keyboard.

title: MUS 317 - Ear Training IV
description: Continues MUS 316.

title: MUS 104 - Exploring Classical Music: Music for Court and Stage
description: Musical elements, styles, techniques, and forms of various musical periods. Lectures, recordings, concerts. Acquire greater understanding and enjoyment of classical music.

title: MUS 301 - Rock: An American Music
description: Major artists and movements of rock music studied in social, historical, and musical contexts. Pioneers of the 50s through today’s rebellion, experimentation, and new trends.

title: MUS 302 - Music in World Culture
description: Explores the musical traditions of African, Indian, Asian, Indonesian, Latin American, and Caribbean cultures compared in artistic, social, religious, and political contexts.

title: MUS 305 - Jazz: An American Art Form
description: Uniquely American art form of jazz. African-American jazz innovators. Perspectives of history, society, performance, gender, and current trends. Lecture, listening, discussion.

title: MUS 444 - History of Music in European Tradition
description: Survey of the history of music in the European Classical tradition.  Focus on the evolution of musical style with an emphasis on the significant musical characteristics of each style period.  Important composers and selected compositions will be examined in detail, while the context in which the music was created will also be studied.

title: MUS 106B - University Singers
description: Study/perform choral literature of many styles and periods. Occasional off-campus concerts. An audition is required.

title: MUS 106E - Opera Workshop
description: Traditional and contemporary musical theatre repertoire: operetta, grand operas, chamber operas, and Broadway musicals. Prepare individual roles, ensembles, and scenes, culminating in public performance. An audition is required.

title: MUS 106F - Mad River Transit Singers
description: Study/perform jazz-idiom choral music. Stylistic interpretation; available literature. Occasional off-campus concerts. An audition is required.

title: MUS 106H - Wind Ensemble
description: Study/perform symphonic band and wind ensemble literature. Occasional off-campus concerts. An audition is required.

title: MUS 106J - AM Jazz Band
description: Performance ensemble for novice jazz instrumentalists. Perform jazz literature; study jazz techniques. An audition is required.

title: MUS 106K - Jazz Orchestra
description: Perform literature composed or arranged for large jazz ensemble. Stylistic interpretation; ensemble playing; study of literature. Occasional off-campus concerts.  There is no formal audition. Consent to enroll is based on interview.

title: MUS 106N - Humboldt Chorale
description: Study/ perform choral music of all periods. Emphasis on larger works. No formal audition. An interview is required.

title: MUS 106O - Humboldt Symphony
description: Study/ perform orchestral literature. Occasional off-campus concerts.

title: MUS 107B - Brass Chamber Music
description: Study/ perform brass chamber music of all eras.

title: MUS 107C - Calypso Band
description: Study/perform traditional and contemporary music for steelband.

title: MUS 107F - Woodwind Chamber Music
description: Study/perform woodwind chamber music of all eras.

title: MUS 107G - Guitar Chamber Music
description: Study/ perform guitar chamber music of all eras.

title: MUS 107H - Mariachi de Humboldt
description: Explore, study, and perform various mariachi styles from Mexico. Open to beginning and experienced performers on violin, trumpet, voice, guitar, vihuela, and guitarrón.

title: MUS 107I - Intermediate Orchestra
description: Study/ perform orchestral music for less experienced players.

title: MUS 107J - Jazz Combos
description: Study/perform jazz combo music from all eras. An audition is required.

title: MUS 107P - Percussion Ensemble
description: Study/ perform traditional and contemporary music for percussion ensemble. An audition is required.

title: MUS 107Q - World Percussion Ensemble
description: Study/perform music for percussion ensembles from around the world.

title: MUS 107S - Chamber Music - Service Learning
description: Study/perform chamber music from all eras. Perform for community partners, and assess and reflect on the experience.

title: MUS 107T - String Chamber Music
description: Study/ perform string chamber music from all eras.

title: MUS 107V - Madrigal Singers
description: Study/perform small ensemble vocal music with emphasis on music of the Renaissance.

title: MUS 353 - Accompanying
description: Keyboard accompanying for instrumental or vocal solos or groups.

title: MUS 319 - Elementary Music Methods
description: Materials and methods for teaching elementary general music in the self-contained K-8 classroom. Designed for preservice music specialists and not for preservice general classroom teachers.

title: MUS 320 - Composition: Film Scoring
description: Study and compose music for scenes of dramatic and narrative films.

title: MUS 320C - Composition: Electronic Music
description: Use computer-driven devices to compose electronic and electro-acoustic music.

title: MUS 323 - Jazz Pedagogy
description: Major principles and concepts involving the teaching of jazz in K-12 classrooms. Emphasis placed on both theory and practice. Music education majors develop skills and strategies for instruction of both instrumental and choral jazz groups.

title: MUS 334 - Fundamentals of Conducting
description: Beat patterns, expressive gestures, score reading, musical ranges, rehearsal planning, correction of errors.

title: MUS 338 - Vocal and Instrumental Scoring
description: Techniques of arranging music for vocal and instrumental performing groups (large and small). Score layout and legibility, part copying, transpositions, and ranges of instruments and voices.

title: MUS 360 - Music Technology
description: Computer technology and music applications. Keyboard controllers and music printing programs.

title: MUS 361 - Music Technology: Recording, Production, and Live Sound
description: Professional audio concepts and techniques in both digital and analog realms. Understanding the recording process from start to finish, studio production, and live sound reinforcement.

title: MUS 384 - Advanced Choral Conducting and Literature
description: Advanced conducting techniques and survey of choral literature for application to K-12 music teaching. Through lecture and physical activity, this course expands on basic conducting patterns and techniques introduced in MUS 334 to include mixed meter, irregular meter, senza misura, cuing, use of left hand, and baton technique. Literature appropriate for major choral ensemble types (concert choir, jazz choir, madrigals, etc.) will be studied.

title: MUS 385P - P / V Performance Seminar
description: Perform, listen to, and critique literature and performances.

title: MUS 387 - Advanced Instrumental Conducting and Literature
description: Advanced conducting techniques and survey of instrumental literature for application to K-12 music teaching. Through lecture and physical activity, this course expands on basic conducting patterns and techniques introduced in MUS 334 to include mixed meter, irregular meter, senza misura, cuing, use of left hand, and baton technique. Literature appropriate for major ensemble types will be studied.

title: MUS 391 - Piano Pedagogy
description: Methods/materials for teaching class and private piano.

title: MUS 391L - Piano Pedagogy Lab
description: Lab-Practice for piano teachers and their students.

title: MUS 392 - Vocal Pedagogy
description: Methods/materials for teaching class and private piano.

title: MUS 392L - Vocal Pedagogy Lab
description: Lab-Practice for voice teachers and their students.

title: MUS 455 - Secondary Music Methods
description: Music education philosophies, methodologies, history, reform movements, significant leaders. Explores the theoretical basis for teaching music in the secondary schools. Rehearsal strategies for K-12 ensembles, Scheduling issues and curriculum in music education. Connecting music with other disciplines.

title: MUS 477W - Career Skills for Performing Artists
description:
Identifying life and career goals in the Arts: finding jobs, writing resumés and cover letters, networking, finances, self-promotion, contracts, mock interviews, and auditions.  This course meets the GWAR requirement.  Students will draft and revise multiple model career-related documents.
Cross-listed with: DANC 477W, TA 477W
